research_series.series_name: Medication and Countermeasure Context Variation Tree

core_interpretation: Medication/countermeasure/procedure logs are context-override layers: new symptoms or physiological shifts should be checked against timing, dose, adherence, interactions and spaceflight PK before assigning a primary mechanism.
guardrails: Do not infer new disease mechanism before checking medication timing.
Do not infer countermeasure failure without adherence/load/timing data.
Do not infer psychiatric or autonomic disorder from medication-induced symptoms.
Do not infer drug causality without temporal relationship.
Do not assume all medications act the same way in space as on Earth.
research_gaps: Lack of systematic inflight pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic studies for most drug classes.
Limited objective tracking of medication adherence/effectiveness during missions.
Insufficient integration of drug interaction/polypharmacy data into operational protocols.
Sparse long-term safety/effectiveness data for hormonal agents in female astronauts.
Need for validated packaging/repackaging strategies for drug stability on long-duration missions.
